body:
# Bug report

## Current behavior

The `NavigationMenu` component adds an `aria-orientation` attribute to the `ul` from `NavigationMenu.List`. This is not appropriate in it's current state because the `ul` element in this situation is assuming the default `list` role which does not support `aria-orientation`.

## Expected behavior

The `role` of `menubar` should be added to the `ul` element, explicitly defining it as a menu bar that contains menu items. This is appropriate because the `NavigationMenu` component implements keyboard interaction pattern that are expected for a `menubar` role. Then, the `aria-orientation` attribute is allowed and does not throw errors in accessibility checks.

Additionally, the `menuitem` role would need to be added to each `NavigationMenu.Item` to satisfy all accessibility checks.

## Reproducible example

The documentation example includes it: https://base-ui.com/react/components/navigation-menu

## Base UI version

1.3.0

## Which browser are you using?

All

## Which OS are you using?

All

## Which assistive tech are you using (if applicable)?

Playwright AXE

comments.body:
This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui/react@4355
```

labels.name: component: navigation menu
labels.description: Changes related to the navigation menu component.

title: [navigation menu] Invalid use of `aria-orientation`
